title: Intensely Marinated Tunisian Doner Meat Tagine
description: A funky and kid-friendly Tunisian dish featuring doner meat in a glassy-jelly sauce, made paleo-friendly and served in individual mini cocottes.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced doner meat to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the meat from the marinade and place it in individual mini cocottes.
Pour the chicken broth into each cocotte, covering the meat completely.
Cover the cocottes with lids and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the lids and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the meat is cooked through.
Remove the cocottes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
